name: Manganese sensor (MntR + CRISPRi)
shortDescription: Detects manganese in water and soil. The native B. subtilis MntR manganese-responsive regulator gates a CRISPRi circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in spore-forming Bacillus subtilis 168.

input.detects: Bioavailable manganese in water and soil

sensing.sensorMechanism: MntR binds Mn(II) and represses manganese-uptake promoters (and activates efflux); metal occupancy switches its target promoter.
sensing.signalFlow: Mn(II) -> MntR switches its target promoter -> transcribes an anti-reporter sgRNA -> CRISPRi inverts a constitutive reporter (NOT). Pair an inverter for turn-on.

parts.name: MntR regulator
parts.notes: Mn(II)-responsive metalloregulator; endogenous to B. subtilis.

parts.name: dCas9
parts.source: Peters et al. 2016 (CRISPRi in B. subtilis)

performance.notes: MntR manganese sensing and B. subtilis CRISPRi are validated separately; integrated device is a design template. Spores allow ambient storage.

safety.notes: Spores allow ambient storage and field reconstitution.

buildSteps.title: Design anti-reporter sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target the sfGFP promoter; check B. subtilis off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: MntR target promoter -> sgRNA (native MntR). TU2: dCas9 + Pveg -> sfGFP. Integrate at amyE.

buildSteps.title: Transform B. subtilis 168
buildSteps.description: Natural competence; select; confirm low background.

buildSteps.title: Expose to sample
buildSteps.description: Reconstitute spores; add sample + manganese standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the native B. subtilis MntR manganese sensor with CRISPRi
partsSources: Que & Helmann / MntR manganese regulation (B. subtilis)
Peters et al. 2016, Cell (CRISPRi in B. subtilis)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
